skill.name: senior-fullstack
skill.description: Fullstack development guidance for React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, architecture patterns, project scaffolding, and code quality review.
skill.summary: Guides fullstack architecture, scaffolding, and code quality workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most were false positives from Markdown code fences, generic headings, and placeholder documentation. No evidence found for malicious intent, network exfiltration, weak cryptography, prompt injection, or dynamic shell execution. The remaining concern is moderate because bundled Python scripts can write JSON output to a user-selected file path when explicitly executed.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected File Write in Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: The bundled Python scripts accept an --output path and write JSON results to that path. This appears legitimate, but it can overwrite files if an agent or user supplies a sensitive destination.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is directly present in all three scripts and uses a caller-provided path. The behavior is opt-in and local, so it is a moderate operational risk rather than confirmed malicious activity.

low_findings.title: Shell Commands Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged shell command patterns in Markdown. Review found command examples for running Python scripts, package managers, Docker, and Kubernetes, not hidden execution by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside fenced bash blocks in SKILL.md. No code path automatically executes these commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography in SKILL.md, reference files, and scripts. Reviewed locations contain generic descriptions, parser descriptions, or placeholder reference text, with no hash or cipher usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic calls. They are natural language or argparse description text.

low_findings.title: Environment File Mention Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: SKILL.md shows a command to copy .env.example to .env during setup. No reviewed script reads environment files or extracts secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment-file evidence is a setup command in a Markdown block. No evidence found of process environment access or credential exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Output Controlled by CLI Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts write JSON reports to args.output when --json and --output are provided. Users should run them in trusted project directories and avoid sensitive output paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed and repeated across all helper scripts. It is not malicious by itself, but it can cause unintended local file writes.

content.user_title: Build Fullstack Apps With Senior Patterns
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent architecture, scaffolding, and code quality guidance across fullstack projects. This skill provides practical workflows for React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL applications.
actual_capabilities: Provides fullstack project guidance for React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL.
Documents architecture patterns, development workflows, and tech stack considerations.
Includes Python helper scripts for scaffold-style analysis and JSON report output.
Lists common development, build, test, lint, Docker, and Kubernetes commands.
Summarizes security, performance, maintainability, and code quality practices.
limitations: Reference documentation is generic and contains placeholder examples.
Helper scripts produce minimal findings and do not implement deep analysis.
The skill does not deploy infrastructure or manage cloud resources directly.
Users must review generated guidance before applying it to production systems.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the stack guidance to outline frontend, backend, database, and deployment choices before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Development Workflows
use_cases.description: Use the workflow notes and command lists to align setup, testing, linting, and review practices.

use_cases.title: Review Fullstack Code Quality
use_cases.description: Use the quality and maintainability guidance to identify gaps in structure, testing, performance, and security.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Stack
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new web app
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a fullstack architecture for a small SaaS app. Include frontend, backend, database, testing, and deployment choices.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define a development workflow for a Next.js and Node.js project. Include setup, quality checks, testing, and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my fullstack project structure. Focus on maintainability, security, performance, testing, and database design risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to produce a production readiness plan for a React, GraphQL, PostgreSQL application. Cover architecture, CI, observability, scaling, and release risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a fullstack architecture for a B2B dashboard.
output_examples.output: A stack recommendation covering Next.js, Node.js APIs, PostgreSQL, authentication, testing layers, deployment flow, and key trade-offs.

output_examples.input: Review our development workflow for a React and GraphQL app.
output_examples.output: A workflow checklist for setup, linting, tests, pull requests, performance checks, security review, and release validation.

output_examples.input: Assess code quality risks before launch.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review summary covering structure, input validation, authorization, dependency hygiene, observability, and missing test coverage.

best_practices: Run helper scripts only on trusted project paths and review output before using it.
Use the guidance as a checklist, then adapt decisions to your product constraints.
Pair architecture recommendations with automated tests, linting, and security review.
anti_patterns: Do not treat placeholder reference examples as production-ready implementation details.
Do not run generated deployment commands against production without manual review.
Do not write script output to sensitive files or untrusted shared directories.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan and review fullstack web applications using React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete production app?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, workflow structure, and simple helper scripts, but implementation still requires developer review.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill access the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes runnable scripts that can write local output files when explicitly invoked with an output path.

faq.question: How should teams use it safely?
faq.answer: Run scripts in trusted repositories, review generated advice, and avoid writing output to sensitive paths.
